mechanism_narrative: A sustained 6-month decline in the federal funds rate signals monetary easing, which compresses short-term rate expectations and, through term structure re-pricing, lowers the 10-year Treasury yield over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Persistent rate declines in easing cycles precede yield drops in historical data
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS falls by 50 bps or more over a 6-month window, 20-day forward yields decline in less than 60% of episodes over a 5-year rolling evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: When year-over-year CPI inflation exceeds 6%, investors demand higher term premiums to compensate for erosion of real returns, pushing the 10-year yield higher over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Historical episodes of CPI > 6% lead to rising bond yields
failure_condition: In instances where CPIAUCSL YoY > 6%, the 20-day yield change is negative in more than 40% of cases over any 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Low unemployment (UNRATE below 4%) combined with accelerating payroll additions (PAYEMS growth > 200,000 per month) signals a tight labor market that is expected to boost wage growth and inflation, thereby lifting the 10-year yield over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Joint tightness measures historically precede yield increases
failure_condition: When UNRATE < 4% and PAYEMS monthly change > 200k, the 20-day yield change is positive in less than 70% of instances over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During post-recession recoveries, a sharp rise in industrial production from a trough signals supply-side normalization, reducing upward inflation pressure and leading to a decline in the 10-year yield over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month change crosses from negative to positive after a trough
evidence_refs: fact:Post-recession rebounds in IP correspond to falling long-term yields in historical cycles
failure_condition: In post-recession periods where INDPRO rises from a trough by >5% over 3 months, 60-day forward yields fall in fewer than 65% of cases over a 10-year historical sample.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in housing starts over a 3-month period signals robust construction demand and rising credit origination, which tends to push up real interest rates and the 10-year yield over the following 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:3-month growth in HOUST historically correlates with rising yields
failure_condition: The rank correlation between the 3-month change in HOUST and the subsequent 20-day yield change is below 0.10 over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly decline in real retail sales (RSAFS drop > 2%) signals an abrupt consumer demand contraction, prompting flight-to-safety buying of Treasuries that lowers the 10-year yield within the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer shocks often trigger safe-haven flows
failure_condition: After a >2% monthly drop in RSAFS, the 5-day forward yield change is negative in fewer than 80% of instances over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous high CPI inflation (above 5% YoY) and very low unemployment (below 4%) creates a stagflationary scare, forcing the Federal Reserve to tighten aggressively and pushing the 10-year yield sharply higher over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:1970s stagflation episodes saw yields surge
failure_condition: When both conditions hold, the 20-day yield change is positive in fewer than 70% of all historical occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: During economic expansions, strong consumer demand growth (RSAFS year-over-year above 3%) signals that the economy is running above potential, increasing the likelihood of Fed tightening and pushing the 10-year yield higher over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Overheating demand phases preceded yield increases in recent cycles
failure_condition: When RSAFS YoY > 3% occurs during an expansion, the 20-day yield change is positive in fewer than 65% of cases over a 3-year rolling window.
